sources.title: Great Expectations by Charles Dickens
sources.rightsBasis: Underlying work is public domain in the UK; Project Gutenberg plain-text edition; only short attributed excerpts are retained.

evidence.locator: Chapter I; Pip meets the escaped convict
evidence.anchor: Keep still, you little devil, or I’ll cut your throat!
And what coarse hands he has! And what thick boots!

evidence.locator: Chapter XVIII; Jaggers announces Pip’s expectations
evidence.anchor: he be immediately removed from his present sphere of life and from this place, and be brought up as a gentleman

evidence.locator: Chapter XLIX; Miss Havisham catches fire
evidence.anchor: she had been in flames, or that the flames were out

evidence.locator: Chapter LIV; Magwitch and Compeyson go overboard
evidence.anchor: they had gone down fiercely locked in each other’s arms

evidence.locator: Chapter LVI; Magwitch dies after learning Estella lives
evidence.anchor: his head dropped quietly on his breast

evidence.locator: Chapter XII; Miss Havisham instructs Estella
evidence.anchor: Break their hearts my pride and hope, break their hearts and have no mercy!
evidence.requiredAnswer: Break their hearts my pride and hope, break their hearts and have no mercy!

evidence.locator: Chapter XXIX; Pip describes loving Estella
evidence.anchor: I loved her against reason, against promise, against peace, against hope, against happiness
evidence.requiredAnswer: I loved her against reason, against promise, against peace, against hope, against happiness

evidence.locator: Chapter XL; Jaggers gives Pip a rule
evidence.anchor: Take nothing on its looks; take everything on evidence.
evidence.requiredAnswer: Take nothing on its looks; take everything on evidence.

evidence.anchor: You have been in every line I have ever read since I first came here
evidence.requiredAnswer: You have been in every line I have ever read since I first came here

evidence.locator: Chapter LIX; Estella reflects on suffering
evidence.anchor: suffering has been stronger than all other teaching
evidence.requiredAnswer: suffering has been stronger than all other teaching

evidence.locator: Chapter LIX; Estella describes her change
evidence.anchor: I have been bent and broken, but—I hope—into a better shape.
evidence.requiredAnswer: I have been bent and broken, but—I hope—into a better shape.
